title: How to watch purchase Evangelion legally in the UK
display_title: How to watch purchase Evangelion legally in the UK
wikitext:
[]: How to watch/purchase Evangelion legally in the UK?             Quote Post by Gretnablue. » Sat Dec 22, 2012 9:57 pm First of all If I have this topic in the wrong section then please tell me. I was recently given the first two Rebuild of Evangelion movies from a friend of mine who knew I had just got into anime. I've only seen a few so far (apart the ones I saw as a kid like Pokemon and Sailor Moon) like Cowboy Bebop, Trigun, Death Note & Hellsing. I enjoyed the films for the most part (though I still don't understand what was with the coffins on the moon at the end of the first one). Now I decided to find the original series but Netflix and Crunchyroll didn't have them and when I checked Amazon and ebay, they were either way to expensive and/or NTSC formatted. Can anyone help?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Dec 23, 2012 1:10 am Not really - you have the choice of waiting until you come across a UK copy you can afford or getting a NTSC copy and finding a way to play it. In fact, as I understand it, the main restriction to playing it is likely to be the region coding, not the PAL/NTSC difference. You can easily circumvent this on a computer with software, or by having two DVD drives set to different regions, and if you want to play it on a separate device that is region-locked, you can rip to new region-free DVDs. My anime collection is a mix of R1, R2 and R4 (my NGE happens to be R2, though), and I just rip them to watch on an iPad (using the program Handbrake) - it has a better picture than my old CRT TV anyway!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Dec 23, 2012 9:10 am Goto Forbidden Planet or some other sci-fi shop.

[]:             Quote Post by Gretnablue. » Sun Dec 23, 2012 4:01 pm Jayfive wrote: Goto Forbidden Planet or some other sci-fi shop.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 23, 2012 4:27 pm It's a bootleg. You can tell from the 26 episodes on 3 disks (i.e. atrocious image quality) and the lack of region coding.

[]:             Quote Post by Gretnablue. » Sun Dec 23, 2012 4:38 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It's a bootleg. You can tell from the 26 episodes on 3 disks (i.e. atrocious image quality) and the lack of region coding.

[]:             Quote Post by mollyblue » Sat Jan 12, 2013 12:33 am Mr. Tines wrote: It's a bootleg. You can tell from the 26 episodes on 3 disks (i.e. atrocious image quality) and the lack of region coding.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Jan 13, 2013 4:14 am As far as I'm aware, the only legit Eva TV releases had either 8 discs (original) or 7 (Platinum).

[]:             Quote Post by Ambrosia » Sun Jan 13, 2013 8:43 pm svenge wrote: As far as I'm aware, the only legit Eva TV releases had either 8 discs (original) or 7 (Platinum).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jan 13, 2013 9:53 pm Ambrosia wrote: If I recall correctly, the Portuguese release from 2000-something had 6 discs.
